title: ADR-008: Persist Staleness to the Database

sections.heading: Context & Problem Statement
sections.content:
Staleness is currently computed on-demand every time it is needed. Two separate code paths produce it:

- `_compute_staleness_from_history()` — reads the `node_history` table to derive status from the last recorded build event. Fast (no file I/O), but blind to any code changes that occurred after the last `cortex build`. Returns CLEAN for nodes that changed post-build.
- `_compute_staleness_rich()` — reads current file content, computes hashes, and compares against stored hashes. Authoritative but runs on every request.

The viz `/api/all` endpoint uses the history path; the viz `/api/check` endpoint, the MCP `cortex_check` tool, and the CLI `cortex check` command all use the rich path. This means the viz dashboard and the MCP/CLI tool routinely disagree — sometimes by a large margin (e.g. 67 CODE_STALE on initial load vs 171 from `cortex check` on the same project).

This creates a trust problem: a developer cannot rely on the graph view staleness rings or summary chips to reflect the actual state of their codebase without manually clicking the Check button first.

sections.heading: The Decision
sections.content:
We will persist the computed staleness status for each node to the database as a `staleness` column on the `nodes` table. All consumers (viz `/api/all`, MCP `cortex_check`, CLI `cortex check`) read from this column. The rich hash-based computation (`_compute_staleness_rich`) remains the only writer.

### Schema change

```sql
ALTER TABLE nodes ADD COLUMN staleness TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'CLEAN';
```

Migration: on first `cortex build` after upgrade, all nodes get staleness written from the rich check. Older DBs without the column fall back to on-demand computation until the next build.

### Write points

Staleness is written to the DB at three points:

1. **`cortex build`** — after discovery and hash computation, write the full staleness map for all nodes.
2. **`cortex check` (CLI/MCP)** — after running `_compute_staleness_rich`, write updated values back.
3. **Viz `/api/check`** — after running `_compute_staleness_rich`, write updated values back (same as CLI check).

### Read points

- **Viz `/api/all`** — reads `staleness` column directly from `nodes` table. No computation on request.
- **MCP `cortex_check`** — reads from `nodes` table. No computation unless `--fresh` flag passed.
- **Viz `/api/check`** — recomputes (rich path), writes back, then returns the updated values.

### The Check button

The Check button and `/api/check` endpoint remain the user-facing action for recomputing staleness against current files. They still run `_compute_staleness_rich` and persist the results. The difference is that the button is now a deliberate refresh action, not a workaround for an inaccurate initial load.

### Background auto-check on viz load

The background `_refreshStaleness()` fire-and-forget added to `_loadProject` can be removed once this ADR is implemented — the initial load will already reflect the last persisted check.

### Vocabulary note (ADR-007 dependency)

This ADR uses the current staleness vocabulary (`CODE_STALE`, `DESC_STALE`, `CONTENT_STALE`, `COVERAGE_STALE`). When ADR-007 is implemented, the stored values will change to the new vocabulary (`CONTENT_STALE`, `DESC_STALE`, `LINKED_STALE`). The schema and write/read logic are identical — only the string values change.

sections.heading: Consequences
sections.content:
**Positive:**
- Single source of truth for staleness — viz, MCP tool, and CLI all read the same value from the DB.
- `/api/all` is instant (pure DB read, no file I/O on request).
- No race conditions between history-path and rich-path results.
- No background fire-and-forget needed on viz page load.
- Staleness survives server restarts — correct on first load after a restart.
- The dashboard summary chips are trustworthy on page load without user action.

**Negative:**
- DB migration required — `ALTER TABLE nodes ADD COLUMN staleness`.
- All existing DBs will show CLEAN until the next `cortex build` or `cortex check` writes the column.
- `_compute_staleness_from_history` becomes dead code and can be removed.

**Neutral:**
- `_compute_staleness_rich` is unchanged — it remains the authoritative computation.
- The Check button UX is unchanged.
- Staleness is still a point-in-time snapshot; it goes stale again as soon as files change after the last write.
